title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Ownership, Access Points, and Public Access · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs iam, bucket policies, ownership, access points, and public access from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for least-privilege S3 authorization proof after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Principal Action Resource Condition · changed case · Review Principal Action…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 8865 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.

Competency probe: Review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.

steps.why: Retrieving Policy Simulation Negative Tests through the concrete least-privilege S3 authorization proof case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Policy Simulation Negative Tests, keep Policy Simulation Negative Tests connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Policy Simulation Negative Tests, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Policy Simulation Negative Tests precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Policy Simulation Negative Tests, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 9056 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Policy Simulation Negative Tests, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: One successful authorized request proves there is no broader unintended access.
